title: satureja capitellata
intro: Satureja capitellata, also known as the Capitellata savory, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is known for its small, white or pink flowers and distinctive, pungent aroma. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. Satureja capitellata can be prepared as a herbal tea, essential oil, or dried herb, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to promote overall well-being.
